does: This paper introduces an agentic commerce protocol that enables ChatGPT to facilitate shopping transactions directly within its interface. The work implements instant checkout functionality, allowing users and AI agents to discover products, negotiate terms, and complete purchases through natural language interaction. The protocol establishes standardized communication patterns between ChatGPT, merchants, and users, enabling the AI to act as an intermediary that understands both natural language intent and commerce requirements. Core contributions include the technical specification for agent-merchant interaction, mechanisms for secure transaction handling, and demonstration of end-to-end shopping workflows from product discovery through payment completion.
connects: This work builds on recent advances in agent-based systems and language model grounding in external tools and APIs. It extends prior research on integrating large language models with structured APIs, drawing from work on tool-use in language models like function calling and plugin architectures. The protocol responds to growing interest in agentic workflows where AI systems orchestrate complex multi-step tasks, similar to recent developments in planning and reasoning in language models. It also connects to research on human-AI collaboration interfaces and the commercialization of AI assistants, building on precedent from voice commerce and chatbot-mediated transactions.
enables: This framework opens pathways toward fully autonomous shopping agents that can negotiate prices, compare alternatives, and execute transactions without human intervention. It establishes infrastructure for richer agent-business interactions, potentially enabling personalized commerce experiences, dynamic pricing based on agent negotiation, and new marketplaces optimized for AI participants. The protocol could support downstream applications including automated procurement systems for businesses, consumer agents that optimize spending across multiple vendors, and integration of commerce into broader agentic workflows. Future work might explore multi-agent auction systems, reputation mechanisms for AI buyers and sellers, and seamless integration of commerce with other agent capabilities like scheduling and information gathering.
